name: Classic Mexican Flan
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt sugar over moderate heat, stirring frequently, until the sugar is a dark caramel-colored liquid. Remove from heat and pour into a 4-cup metal ring mold or 8 individual molds. Quickly turn the mold to coat the bottom and sides with the caramel. Let cool so that the caramel hardens.
2. Preheat the oven to 325°F (160°C). In an electric blender, combine the sweetened condensed milk, milk, egg yolks, and extracts. Cover and blend until well mixed.
3. Pour the mixture into the mold(s) and place them in a larger pan filled with water to a depth of about 1/2 inch. Bake for 1 hour. Remove from the oven and let cool. Refrigerate up to 2 days.
4. Before serving, cover the mold with an inverted serving platter. Hold both together and gently turn them over to lift off the mold. The caramel will fall as a liquid sauce over the custard.
